_comment: ADOPTER EXAMPLE — architecture assurance A1–A5 (Loom 2.0 §7.2). Every A2 threat traces to a control and a test; a material open finding blocks backlog creation.

A1-data-privacy.inventory: income, obligations, bureau score — classified per docs/governance/data-lifecycle.json
A1-data-privacy.residency: UAE-resident stores only; no cross-border model traffic
A1-data-privacy.retention: per data-lifecycle manifest; erasure via crypto-shred

threats.threat: Prompt injection via repository content steers the delivery agent
threats.control: pii-guard + spec/test tripwires + protected control plane (HG-0002)

threats.threat: Agent weakens tests to reach green
Evidence forgery in the release bundle
Credential leakage into repo or history

threats.control: model-provenance gate: pinned eval with sealed report + independent validation

A3-operational-resilience.degraded_mode: manual underwriting queue; decisioning suspended, not improvised
A3-operational-resilience.recovery: stateless decision service; RTO 4h target pending R-gate machinery (1.12)

A4-model-risk.purpose: affordability assessment in the credit-limit decision path
A4-model-risk.human_override: underwriter can override with reason code; overrides monitored
A4-model-risk.monitoring_plan: monthly drift + override-rate review by model risk
A4-model-risk.suspension: model-risk function can suspend to manual underwriting

A5-financial-integrity.maker_checker: limit increases above threshold require checker approval
A5-financial-integrity.reconciliation: daily limits-vs-ledger reconciliation; breaks page operations
A5-financial-integrity.audit: INSERT-only audit trail with lineage emission (Q4.5)

findings.note: Bureau sandbox lacks latency parity; accepted for delivery, revisit at PA2.
